response.justification: Bogdan is a strong match for this role, bringing 15+ years of full-stack engineering with deep TypeScript and Node.js experience across multiple production-scale environments. His current position at re:cinq directly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which maps precisely to the AI agent development requirement — a differentiator most candidates will lack. He has a well-established TDD culture backed by open-source testing libraries and public workshops, and his cloud and containerization experience (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes) is solid. The main gaps are the absence of explicit NestJS experience and limited evidence of React depth, both of which are primary stack requirements.
strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js background across multiple production roles including real-time, high-throughput, and pricing systems
Current AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq directly satisfies the AI agent development requirement
Strong, demonstrated TDD and testing culture: open-source testing libraries, Selenium courses, and repeated workshop facilitation
concerns: NestJS is not mentioned anywhere in the resume despite being listed as a primary stack technology
React experience appears only once (Optilyz) with no detail on component architecture, state management, or ecosystem depth
Kotlin is absent; Java appears only in older/embedded contexts, making the secondary backend stack coverage thin

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP tooling experience
